title: Queens Destination Run
description: With Queens Marathon Weekend 26 weeks away, we are promoting our Borough with the return of our @queensdistance Destination Run Series. Marathon training will be in full swing, and we wanted to start off with a distance that is manageable, easy to navigate on a map (a straight line heading south on Woodhaven Blvd), and […]

title: Jackson Heights Greenmarket
address: 34th Ave & 79th Street &, 80th St, Jackson Heights, NY 11372, USA
description: Known as our Queens flagship, the Jackson Heights Greenmarket has connected local farms and farmers to the residents of this Jackson Heights neighborhood since 2000. Every Sunday, year-round, families, long-time residents and young couples can find a diverse selection of locally-grown and produced foods throughout the market. Beyond shopping, visitors can enjoy cooking demonstrations, educational […]

title: Les Petites Gallery
description: Les Petites Gallery is LIVE NYC’s only permitted Open Air Art Gallery on an Open Street. Les Petites Gallery hits the street every Sunday May 17 — Dec 20 | 9am-6pm 34th Ave between 82nd & 83rd St | Near the 7 train

title: Pages in the Park: This is Not a Small Voice with Traci N. Todd
description: Every Sunday at 11AM in Travers Park at the amphitheater authors and illustrators will come together to read their books aloud, bringing stories to life in the heart of our neighborhood.

title: LaGuardia Float in Queens Pride
description: Join LaGuardia students, faculty, and staff in the Queens Pride Parade with a float provided by the Bindlestiff Family Cirkus.

title: March with Jackson Heights Indivisible at Queens Pride Parade 2026
description: Join Jackson Heights Indivisible in the Queens Pride Parade on June 7 as we march together in solidarity and celebration. All are welcome. Come ready to walk, dance, wave, and show support for LGBTQ+ rights and community in Queens. Bring water and wear comfortable shoes, hats, and sunscreen. Meetup details and logistics will be shared […]

title: AI for the People
description: An AI community summit featuring live demos from civic innovators, expert panels, and hands-on workshops.AI For the People is a free, community-centered AI summit hosted by Epicenter NYC at the Museum of the Moving Image, bringing together civic innovators, community leaders, journalists, and technologists to explore how artificial intelligence can better serve our communities.Through live […]

title: Queens Pride Parade & Festival
description: The Queens Pride Parade and Multicultural Festival is the second oldest and second largest pride parade in New York City. Founded in 1993, it is held annually on the first Sunday in June, in the neighborhood of Jackson Heights. The parade was founded by Daniel Dromm and co-chaired by Maritza Martinez to raise the visibility of the […]

title: XXXI Free Thalia Community Festival
description: Flamenco Music & Dance with Danza España
